items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive US Futures Lower as Oil Surges
items.summary: US stock index futures are pointing to a significantly lower open on Monday, March 2, 2026, as escalating tensions in the Middle East, including US-Israeli strikes on Iran and the resulting surge in crude oil prices, rattle global markets. Investors are shifting towards safe-haven assets amidst increased risk aversion.

items.statContext: S&P 500 E-mini futures fell around 1% in early trading, breaking below a key technical support level.
Nasdaq E-mini futures also dropped around 1% due to geopolitical concerns and ongoing sensitivity to AI capex concerns.
Dow Jones Industrial Average futures declined approximately 1% as global risk aversion mounted.

items.headline: Upstream Oil Producers Expected to Gain
items.summary: Upstream oil producers such as $ONGC and Oil India may see positive sentiment and improved realizations as crude oil prices surge due to Middle East tensions.

items.headline: Defense Stocks Could See Positive Sentiment
items.summary: Companies in the defense sector, including $HAL and $BEL, may experience positive sentiment amidst the escalating geopolitical conflict.

items.headline: Oil Marketing Companies Under Pressure
items.summary: Sectors sensitive to crude prices, such as oil marketing companies, paints, tyres, aviation, and chemicals, are likely to face pressure on margins due to rising input costs.

items.headline: Tech and Financial Sectors Continue to Face Headwinds
items.summary: Following declines on the previous trading day, tech and financial sectors are expected to remain under pressure, as investors trim exposure to crowded AI and growth trades.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Escalates, Driving Risk Aversion
items.summary: Joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran over the weekend, resulting in the death of Iran's Supreme Leader, have significantly heightened geopolitical risks. This escalation is a primary factor behind the broad market sell-off and flight to safety.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Soar Amid Supply Concerns
items.summary: Brent crude prices jumped by as much as 13% in early trading, reaching a 14-month high of $82 per barrel, as the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz intensifies concerns over global oil supplies.

items.headline: Gold Rises as Investors Seek Safety
items.summary: Gold futures saw a rise of approximately 2% as investors moved into safe-haven assets in response to the heightened global uncertainty.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Expected Today
items.summary: The US economic calendar for March 2, 2026, includes the ISM Manufacturing Index, Construction Spending SA M/M, ISM Manufacturing Price SA, and ISM Manufacturing SA, all scheduled for release.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports in Focus This Week
items.summary: While no major US earnings are expected before market open today, the first week of March is anticipated to be busy with reports from companies in cybersecurity, retail, AI, and e-commerce, including $CRWD, $TGT, $AVGO, $OKTA, $RGTI, $COST, and $JD.
